core_mechanism: flat-histogram adaptive Monte Carlo estimation of a density of states (Wang-Landau algorithm): a random walk over the values of a scalar quantity that iteratively reweights itself to penalize already-visited values, forcing the visitation histogram flat and thereby recovering the full entropy/density-of-states function of the system in a single run
brief_reason: The abstract states the results are obtained via a two-stage strategy of entropic sampling in dominant energy subspaces using mainly the Wang-Landau algorithm, i.e. flat-histogram estimation of the density of states is the machinery on which the whole method rests.
